topIssues: Frame 3 & 4: These frames are completely identical, resulting in 1.8 seconds of static 'thinking' state with no visual progression in either the chat, spreadsheet, or room trace.
Frame 9 & 10: These frames are also identical, creating a redundant frame split at the end of the GIF instead of a single held frame.

Frames 5-8: The sequential CAS writes update every 900ms. This is too fast for a first-time viewer to simultaneously track the green variance value appearing in the sheet and the corresponding log entry appearing in the 'Room trace' at the bottom right.
fixSuggestions: Delete the duplicate Frame 4 to eliminate the dead air, or add a pulsing/active loading state to the 'thinking' indicator to show active processing.
Merge Frame 9 and Frame 10 into a single final frame with a combined duration of 6210ms to clean up the GIF timeline.
Increase the pacing of the write steps (Frames 5-8) from 900ms to 1200ms to give the viewer's eyes enough time to scan both the spreadsheet updates and the room trace logs.
